meta.title: TezAtlas — Agentic Academic Workflow Framework
meta.description: From first source to final submission — phase-gated, source-anchored, AI-guided academic workflow engine.

hero.headline: From first source to final submission.

problem.title: AI writing tools hallucinate citations.
problem.description: Papers that don't exist. Authors who never wrote them. Page numbers from nowhere.
problem.point1: Generative AI tools produce convincing-looking but entirely fabricated academic references.
problem.point2: Starting to write before the literature is sufficiently read, claims without source backing, and missed feedback cycles.
problem.point3: Thesis text without version control cannot be safely revised, recovered after a crash, or audited by an advisor.
problem.point4: Sources you know exist but haven't read — shadow literature that silently weakens an argument you think is fully supported.
problem.solution: TezAtlas can't. Iron Rule 1 makes citation fabrication structurally impossible — not by warning, not by prompt, but by hard constraint.

features.title: Designed with the discipline that academic research demands.
features.subtitle: TezAtlas is not a chatbot. It is a structured, phase-gated, source-anchored workflow engine.
features.f1: Just as Git requires a commit before a push, TezAtlas requires reading saturation before writing, a revision cycle, and review before submission.
features.f2: Inviolable constraints that apply to every session, every document type, and every language. Cannot be overridden by user, document type, or deadline pressure.
features.f3: Real-time context tracking that treats each paragraph as a linked node in a semantic chain, not an isolated block.
features.f4: AI vocabulary blacklist, burstiness control, and writing style profile — eliminates AI fingerprint to preserve the author's authentic voice.
features.f5: Source Hunter, Methodology Checker, and Citation Verifier — specialized external LLM calls for tasks that benefit from parallel execution.
features.f6: PyMuPDF for text-layer PDFs, Tesseract for scanned documents. No GPU, no API key — works on any machine.
features.f7: In Draft Generator mode, every AI-generated sentence carries an invisible provenance tag with source and reasoning.
features.f8: For every major argument node: strongest supporting source and strongest counter-evidence — mandatory before writing phase.
features.f9: Claim extraction, gap detection, and contradiction detection — runs passively during reading and writing phases.
features.f10: Field-specific methodology, citation format, and source hierarchy overrides for Law, Medicine, STEM, Social Sciences, and Humanities.
features.f11: Discipline-specific workflows, institution-specific formatting requirements, and field-specific technique nodes — packaged and distributable.
features.f12: Every session ends with a structured state snapshot that lets the next session rebuild context without re-explanation.

howItWorks.title: Three commands. Zero configuration.
howItWorks.step3: 5-question onboarding — document type, language, field, mode, and researcher type
howItWorks.detail: TezAtlas routes you to the correct Phase 0 based on your selections, loads only the skill nodes relevant to that phase, and begins the guided workflow. No configuration files to edit. No environment variables to set.

modes.title: Who holds the pen changes — the discipline stays constant.
modes.mode1desc: The trainer who spots you, not the one who lifts for you.
modes.mode1detail: You write. The AI questions, scaffolds, checks, and challenges. The AI never generates your core argument, your thesis statement, or your interpretive claims.
modes.mode2desc: The chef who preps ingredients — you still cook the dish.
modes.mode2detail: The AI drafts from your notes and sources. You review, substantially revise, and own the output. Every sentence is auditable via provenance tags.
modes.hardRule: Both modes enforce all 9 Iron Rules identically. Source fabrication is impossible in either mode.

docTypes.title: 10 academic output types. Each with its own phase architecture.
docTypes.subtitle: Every document type has unique phase gates, methodology forks, and quality checkpoints.
docTypes.thesisDesc: Saturation-based reading gate, empirical methodology fork, defense armor
docTypes.articleDesc: Peer review revision cycle, journal selection phase, contribution claim gate
docTypes.conferenceDesc: Starts with abstract, deadline motor, presentation prep
docTypes.litReviewDesc: PRISMA + PROSPERO, data extraction phase, meta-analysis path
docTypes.reportDesc: Executive summary drafted first, dissemination phase, stakeholder-aware writing
docTypes.bookChapterDesc: Volume alignment gate, editor-reviewer cycle, final integration
docTypes.grantDesc: Funder analysis phase, budget justification, compliance checklist
docTypes.proposalDesc: Gate to thesis: approved proposal required before thesis begins
docTypes.posterDesc: Extreme content compression, visual hierarchy planning before writing
docTypes.technicalDesc: Falsifiable hypothesis gate, strict reproducibility and environment tracking

ironRules.title: Inviolable constraints. No exceptions.
ironRules.subtitle: Each rule closes a specific, well-documented failure mode in AI-assisted academic work.
ironRules.r1: No sentence is written and no citation is made without its source physically present in the /sources/ directory.
ironRules.r2: Every source's footnotes and bibliography are scanned. Relevant cited works must be found, evaluated, and included or explicitly excluded with a reason.
ironRules.r3: When a source is needed and doesn't exist in /sources/, TezAtlas attempts to locate it before asking the user.
ironRules.r4: Citations from AI training memory are forbidden. Guessing page numbers is forbidden. Every citation must be verified against a file in /sources/.
ironRules.r5: Before every phase transition, a supervisor or advisor checkpoint is required. A structured template documents what was reviewed and approved.
ironRules.r6: Every TezAtlas session ends with a Git commit. Not 'when it feels right.' Every session, every time, without exception.
ironRules.r7: Any blocker — missing source, unanswered question, unresponsive advisor — is escalated immediately with a concrete action.
ironRules.r8: Before writing phase: for every major argument node, strongest supporting source and strongest counter-source.
ironRules.r9: Sources deferred during reading are reviewed before writing begins. Writing cannot start until the deferred pool is empty.

techStack.title: ~130 interconnected skill nodes. No monolithic prompt.
techStack.subtitle: The right knowledge at the right moment. The full skill graph is never loaded into context simultaneously.
techStack.detail: Each node is ~80 lines of focused content. Bilingual (English and Turkish) with YAML frontmatter metadata and wikilink-style references. Phase 3's nodes are released when Phase 4 begins.
techStack.providersDetail: Any OpenAI-compatible LLM provider works out of the box. Gemini, DeepSeek, OpenAI, Grok, Groq, Ollama, and Anthropic.

disciplines.title: Field-specific methodology, citation, and source hierarchy.
disciplines.lawDesc: OSCOLA, Bluebook, Yargıtay/AYM/Danıştay formats. IRAC and subsumption argument structures. Kazancı and Legalbank integration.
disciplines.medicineDesc: CONSORT/PRISMA/STROBE reporting guidelines. IRB ethics documentation. Vancouver citation. Clinical trial registration.
disciplines.stemDesc: LaTeX export, GitHub reproducibility, arXiv/bioRxiv preprint protocol. IEEE citation. Docker environment reproducibility.
disciplines.socialDesc: APA 7, qualitative transparency, survey validation. Positionality statement, codebook generation, transferability criteria.
disciplines.humanitiesDesc: Chicago 17, MLA 9, archival research. Primary vs secondary source hierarchy. Close reading and discourse analysis.

research.title: Not intuitive guesswork. Every structural decision traces to documented research findings.
research.r1finding: 40–50% PhD attrition driven by isolation and lack of structured progress visibility.
research.r1response: Risk signal detection, progress visibility dashboard, escalation protocol
research.r2finding: Daily writing in small sessions outperforms binge writing in productivity and quality.
research.r2response: Streak tracker, session scheduler, session-level Git commit as minimum unit
research.r3finding: Self-regulated learning cycle: forethought → performance → reflection.
research.r3response: Pre-session goal-setting ritual, active phase work, post-session reflection and commit
research.r4finding: Expert writers plan for their reader before drafting; novices plan for themselves.
research.r4response: Reader-impact prompts embedded in every writing phase

roadmap.title: tezatlas.com — Hosted Version
roadmap.subtitle: TezAtlas framework is free and open source. tezatlas.com will offer a managed version for researchers who want the workflow without the setup.
roadmap.phase1desc: Searchable skill graph documentation, phase flow diagrams, installation guides.
roadmap.phase2desc: Bring your own API key. Zero setup. TezAtlas in the browser. Built-in PDF processing.
roadmap.phase3desc: Shared /sources/ library, parallel reading, co-author checkpoints.
roadmap.phase4desc: Community skill packs, discipline modules, agent config marketplace.
roadmap.byok: BYOK Model: You bring your own Anthropic API key. We supply the workflow. No AI costs billed through us.

session.title: Research attrition is rarely intellectual — it is motivational and structural.
session.srlDesc: Every session has three mandatory structural components: Forethought (goal-setting), Performance (active work), and Reflection (what was produced, what blocked, next goal + Git commit).
session.streakDesc: Scheduled, protected writing time. Recommended session: 45-90 minutes. Sessions exceeding 3 hours trigger a warning. Three consecutive missed sessions trigger Iron Rule 7 escalation.
session.dashboardDesc: ASCII progress bars with visual proximity. Proximity to a completed phase accelerates effort in the final approach.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions.
faq.q1: Is TezAtlas an AI writing tool?
faq.a1: No. TezAtlas is a workflow engine. It doesn't write for you — it structures your writing process with the discipline that academic research demands. In Thought Partner mode, the AI never generates your core argument, thesis statement, or interpretive claims.
faq.q2: Which LLM providers does it support?
faq.a2: Any OpenAI-compatible LLM provider works: Gemini, DeepSeek, OpenAI, Grok, Groq, Ollama. Anthropic is supported via separate SDK. Configure once, every agent routes to your chosen provider.
faq.q3: Does it require GPU or special hardware?
faq.a3: No. Works on any machine that can run Python 3.8+ and Claude Code. No GPU needed for the OCR pipeline. Even runs on a Raspberry Pi 5.
faq.a4: Turkish and English are primary languages. German, French, and Spanish OCR support is available. Skill nodes are bilingual (English and Turkish).
faq.a5: MIT License. Free and open source. Community plugins are under CC BY 4.0.
faq.a6: In planning phase. Static documentation site first, then BYOK web app. Early access list will open at tezatlas.com.

team.title: Behind TezAtlas
team.missionText: To empower researchers worldwide with a workflow engine that builds capability, preserves ownership, and guarantees academic rigor.
team.visionText: In the age of generative noise, TezAtlas is the signal. It transforms the lonely, often chaotic journey of research into a disciplined, agentic, and source-anchored process.
team.nameOriginText: Tez (Turkish): The core argument to be defended. Atlas: A structured record of explored intellectual territory, the instrument of navigation.
team.builtOn: Built on Raspberry Pi 5 · Powered by Agentic AI · Mapping the future of human inquiry.

cta.title: Discover the future of academic writing.
cta.subtitle: Free. Open source. MIT licensed.
cta.waitlistDesc: Be the first to know when the hosted version is ready.
cta.waitlistSuccess: You're on the list. Thank you!

testimonials.title: What researchers say.
testimonials.t1quote: Thanks to Iron Rule 1, I never question my citations anymore. Every sentence has a physical file behind it.
testimonials.t2quote: Phase gates kept me in the reading phase. I used to start writing immediately — now I don't pick up the pen until I pass the saturation gate.
testimonials.t3quote: The OCR pipeline processed over 200 scanned PDFs automatically. The fact that it runs even on a Raspberry Pi is incredible.
testimonials.note: * Early user feedback. Real names and institutions have been changed for privacy.

security.title: Your data stays with you.
security.subtitle: TezAtlas is designed with a local-first architecture. Your sources, notes, and thesis text are never sent to our servers.
security.localDesc: All source files, notes, and thesis text stay in the local /sources/ directory. No cloud sync.
security.noCloudDesc: TezAtlas runs inside Claude Code. Your API key stays client-side — never passes through our servers.
security.auditDesc: Every AI-generated sentence is tagged with a provenance label. Source and reasoning are always traceable.
security.gitDesc: Every session ends with a mandatory Git commit. Full revision history, recovery, and advisor audit.

footer.tagline: From first source to final submission — phase-gated, source-anchored, AI-guided.
